papers.title: SPIROMICS: design and protocol for a multi-site study of COPD
papers.journal: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine
papers.access_route: BioLINCC application (biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov); SPIROMICS materials available via request to NHLBI
papers.notes: n=2900 main cohort; ~250 underwent bronchoscopy with BAL collection; K2EDTA plasma + buffy at -80C; multi-site US; GOLD stage 0-4 documented; smoking history detailed

papers.title: Characterization of the lower airway microbiota and inflammatory response in COPD: a SPIROMICS study
papers.journal: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine
papers.notes: Blood and BAL matched from same visit; immunophenotyping by flow cytometry; microbiome profiling from BAL; SPIROMICS bronchoscopy sub-cohort

papers.title: BAL metabolomics in COPD: a SPIROMICS substudy
papers.journal: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine
papers.notes: Matched BAL + plasma from n=115 SPIROMICS participants; metabolomics assay performed; demonstrates BAL metabolite readout feasibility; GOLD stage and FEV1 documented

papers.title: UAB Pulmonary Biorepository: design and initial results
papers.institution: University of Alabama at Birmingham (UAB) Pulmonary Biorepository
papers.access_route: Collaborative agreement with UAB Division of Pulmonary, Allergy and Critical Care Medicine; IRB required
papers.notes: Single-center biorepository with BAL, plasma, and buffy coat matched per subject; COPD and asthma subjects; spirometry and imaging documented; contact via Dr. J. Edwin Blalock or successors

expected_retrievable_institutions: University of Alabama at Birmingham Pulmonary Biorepository
notes: SPIROMICS is the dominant source via BioLINCC with K2EDTA plasma, buffy, and BAL all matched. UAB provides a complementary single-center collection. All three SPIROMICS papers draw from the same cohort — treat as one institution for deduplication.
